mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a labor market inflection that typically precedes corporate earnings downgrades, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand-supply contraction, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and HOUST does not predict negative 60d forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising nominal rates tighten financial conditions faster than earnings growth can compensate, suppressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high inflation periods do not show a negative correlation with rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in strong payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer credit growth falls below 500000 million dollars, it signals a credit crunch that restricts household spending and corporate working capital, driving negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS drops below 500000.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and stagnant GDP growth indicates supply-side bottlenecks resolving while demand weakens, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and GDPC1 growth rates does not predict negative 60d forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, rising unemployment triggers wage-price spiral fears that force aggressive Fed tightening, rapidly compressing equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising UNRATE combined with rising FEDFUNDS does not predict negative 5d forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
